verbGlossary.ABSORBS: {source} provision provides a monetary cushion that absorbs cost impact when {target} materializes, preventing budget overrun (e.g., allowance absorbs design-risk scope gaps discovered after award)
verbGlossary.ASSIGNS: {source} action designates a responsible party who owns the associated {target} going forward (e.g., award notice assigns risk owner for the contracted scope)
verbGlossary.BACKSTOPS: {source}provides a financial guarantee that protects against {target} if the responsible party defaults (e.g., performance bond backstops construction execution risk if subcontractor abandons work)
verbGlossary.BOUNDS: {source} exhibit defines the limits within which {target} is contained, making scope clear and gaps identifiable (e.g., scope of work bounds design risk by specifying included and excluded items)
verbGlossary.COVERS: {source}provides financial protection for losses caused by a specific {target} (e.g., builders risk policy covers environmental risk damage to the structure during construction)
verbGlossary.DEFINES: {source} document establishes the contractual terms that determine how {target} is distributed among parties (e.g., bid package defines risk allocation between GC and subcontractors)
verbGlossary.ENFORCES: {source} penalty clause compels {target} by imposing financial consequences for risk events (e.g., liquidated damages clause enforces schedule mitigation by penalizing late delivery)
verbGlossary.EXPOSES TO: {source} instrument or procurement action creates vulnerability to {target} category through its structure or requirements (e.g., purchase order exposes project to supply chain risk when committing to a sole-source supplier)
verbGlossary.FEEDS: {source} provides input data to {target} assessment or tracking framework (e.g., insurance compliance rate feeds risk mitigation completion tracking)
verbGlossary.GOVERNS: {source} establishes the overarching {target} framework that controls how risk is allocated across all project parties (e.g., prime contract governs risk allocation between owner and general contractor)
verbGlossary.HELD AGAINST: {source} retention amount is withheld as security against {target} until obligations are fulfilled (e.g., retainage terms held against residual risk of incomplete punch list work)
verbGlossary.INFORMS: {source} data provides decision-making input to {target} assessment (e.g., bid evaluation results inform likelihood assessment of cost overrun based on bidder spread)
verbGlossary.MITIGATES: {source} or contractual mechanism reduces the probability or impact of {target} (e.g., prequalification mitigates construction execution risk by filtering out unqualified subcontractors)
verbGlossary.MIRRORS: {source} and {target} track the same underlying phenomenon from different perspectives (e.g., contract change order rate mirrors risk change order rate as leading indicator)
verbGlossary.PENALIZES: {source} clause imposes financial consequences when a specific {target} occurs, incentivizing prevention (e.g., liquidated damages penalize schedule risk by assessing daily charges for late completion)
verbGlossary.PRESCRIBES: {source} terms specify required {target} response procedures that parties must follow when risks materialize (e.g., general conditions prescribe risk response protocols for unforeseen site conditions)
verbGlossary.SATISFIES: {source}document fulfills a contractual risk transfer requirement (e.g., insurance certificate satisfies the insurance requirement specified in {target})
verbGlossary.SCREENS BY: {source} qualification process evaluates parties against {target} criteria before engagement (e.g., prequalification screens subcontractors by risk score based on safety record and financial stability)
verbGlossary.SCREENS FOR: {source} evaluation process specifically checks for exposure to {target} category (e.g., prequalification screens for safety risk by reviewing EMR and OSHA history)
verbGlossary.SECURES VIA: {source}provides bonded financial security through a surety bond mechanism (e.g., bid bond secures the owner via surety bond against bidder withdrawal)
verbGlossary.SIGNALS: {source} serves as an early warning indicator that {target} is emerging or worsening (e.g., declining subcontractor retention rate signals labor risk on future projects)
verbGlossary.TRANSFERS: {source} commitment moves {target} responsibility to another party through contractual obligation (e.g., subcontract transfers labor risk to the subcontractor for their crew)
verbGlossary.TRANSFERS VIA: {source} instrument serves as the contractual vehicle through which {target} is formally allocated to another party (e.g., subcontract transfers risk via risk allocation clauses in the agreement)
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: {source} milestone or event activates {target} condition or response (e.g., substantial completion triggers risk transfer from contractor to owner for the completed portion)
